title: Mailchimp vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Mailchimp and Constant Contact. Find pricing, features, and which email marketing platform suits your business best.
h1: Mailchimp vs Constant Contact: Detailed Comparison
intro: Mailchimp and Constant Contact are two established email marketing platforms with different approaches to serving businesses. Mailchimp emphasizes affordability with a free tier, while Constant Contact focuses on all-in-one marketing tools for local businesses and nonprofits. Understanding their key differences will help you choose the right platform for your marketing needs.

comparison_table.tool_b: Event management, SMS, social ads, e-commerce

choose_a_if: You want to start for free with up to 500 subscribers
You're a beginner looking for an easy-to-use drag-and-drop editor
Budget is your primary concern and you need affordable automation
choose_b_if: You need SMS marketing and social media ad integration
You run an e-commerce store and need built-in shopping features
You manage events or require advanced list-building tools

faq.q: Is Mailchimp better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: Neither is universally better—it depends on your needs. Mailchimp excels for budget-conscious beginners with its free tier and strong automation, scoring higher on G2 (4.3 vs 4.0). Constant Contact offers more advanced features like SMS, social ads, and e-commerce, making it better for established businesses needing comprehensive marketing tools.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Mailchimp or Constant Contact?
faq.a: Mailchimp is significantly cheaper if you're starting out, with a free tier supporting 500 subscribers at no cost. Constant Contact has no free tier and starts at $12/month. For paid plans, Mailchimp begins at $13/month, making both competitively priced once you exceed Mailchimp's free limit.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to Mailchimp?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support email list exports and imports. You can export your contacts from Constant Contact as a CSV file and import them into Mailchimp. However, you'll lose any automation workflows or advanced segmentation, so plan the migration carefully to minimize disruption.
